Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Looking at the sequences in the event log Case 1 OR to MS to QS to AS to IQC to AS to TST to RT to TST to PK to LB to DP to D Case 2 OR to MS to QS to AS to IQC to AS to TST to PK to DP to D Case 3 OR to MS to QS to AS to IQC to IQC to AS to IQC to AS to TST to RT to TST to PK to LB to DP to D Case 4 OR to MS to QS to AS to TST to PK to LB to DP to D Case 5 OR to MS to QS to AS to IQC to AS to IQC to AS to TST to RT to TST to RT to TST to PK to LB to DP to D

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: OR to MS to QS to AS This sequence is strictly ordered and appears in all cases So OR to MS to QS to AS

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: After AS we either have IQC or we go straight to TST

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1 AS to IQC to AS to TST Case 5 AS to IQC to AS to IQC to AS to TST This looks like a loop where the main activity is AS and IQC is part of the loop

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: If A equals AS B equals IQC then it is AS then IQC AS repeated

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 AS to IQC to AS matches Case 5 AS to IQC to AS to IQC to AS matches Case 4 AS to TST matches

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: What about Case 3s AS to IQC to IQC to AS If I use AS loop with IQC choice it cannot generate IQC to IQC

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: How to generate AS to IQC to IQC to AS Option A as loop equals LOOP with children AS and iqc loop

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: iqc loop equals LOOP with children IQC and skip This generates one or more IQC activities Then as loop equals LOOP with children AS and iqc loop This means execute AS then either exit or execute one or more IQC and then AS again

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This perfectly matches Case 1 AS to IQC to AS Case 2 AS to IQC to AS Case 3 AS to IQC to IQC to AS to IQC to AS Case 4 AS exit immediately Case 5 AS to IQC to AS to IQC to AS

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: TST to RT to TST in Case 1 and Case 3 and TST to RT to TST to RT to TST in Case 5 This is clearly a loop

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: tst loop equals LOOP with children TST and RT This models execute TST then either exit or execute RT and then TST again

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: After PK we have LB in some cases and skip in Case 2 so after PK we have an exclusive choice between LB and skip and then DP then D

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: lb choice equals XOR with children LB and skip and the sequence after testing is PK to lb choice to DP to D

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: There is no evidence of concurrency in the event log All activities happen one after another in a single thread per case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So a single StrictPartialOrder with a linear chain of edges is appropriate nodes OR MS QS as loop tst loop PK lb choice DP D with edges OR to MS MS to QS QS to as loop as loop to tst loop tst loop to PK PK to lb choice lb choice to DP DP to D

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 4 OR to MS to QS to AS to TST to PK to LB to DP to D My model allows this perfectly Case 2 and Case 5 are also matched perfectly
